content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

October 7, 1994

Stephen R. Oettinger
Director of Community Relations and Public Affairs
ARCO Transportation Company
300 Oceangate
Long Beach, CA 90802-4341

Re: Your Request For Advice
Our File No. A-94-295

Dear Mr. Oettinger:

We confirm the advice you received from this office by
telephone on September 1, 1994, regarding documentation and
disclosure of contributions and expenditures for Mayor Beverly
O'Neill's inaugural celebration.

You stated that a number of corporations and individuals have
made contributions to the Mayor's campaign fund and that a portion
of that fund will be used for an inaugural celebration. You
inquired whether or not the campaign account would be the proper
repository for contributed funds that were specifically designated
for the inaugural and whether or not an inaugural celebration
could be financed with campaign account funds.

The Political Reform Act (the "Act"),¹ requires that all
contributions received by a candidate or a candidate's controlled
committee be deposited into the campaign account. (Section
85201.) A contribution is any payment given without full and
adequate consideration unless it is clear from the surrounding
circumstances that it is not given for political purposes.
(Section 82015.) A contribution earmarked for inaugural costs
would be a contribution for political purposes which would be
required to be deposited into the candidate's campaign account.

Generally, a candidate may use the funds in the campaign
account for any expenditure reasonably related to a political
purpose. (Section 89512.) Any expenditure which confers a
substantial personal benefit on the candidate or any other person

__________________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000-18954. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File No. A-94-295
Page 2


with the ability to authorize the expenditure of campaign funds
must be directly related to a political, legislative or
governmental purpose. (Id.) The Act also enumerates certain
specific activities for which expenditure of campaign funds are
allowed; among the listed activities is the expenditure for an
election victory celebration or similar campaign event. (Section
89513(f)(2).) Accordingly, an inaugural celebration would be a
permitted use of campaign funds.

We trust that this advice adequately addresses the concerns
raised in your telephone request and follow-up letter. Should you
have any further questions regarding application of the Act, this
office remains available to assist you.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Daniel E. Muallem
Counsel, Legal Division

sections.question: You stated that a number of corporations and individuals have made contributions to the Mayor's campaign fund and that a portion of that fund will be used for an inaugural celebration. You inquired whether or not the campaign account would be the proper repository for contributed funds that were specifically designated for the inaugural and whether or not an inaugural celebration could be financed with campaign account funds.
sections.conclusion: A contribution earmarked for inaugural costs would be a contribution for political purposes which would be required to be deposited into the candidate's campaign account. Generally, a candidate may use the funds in the campaign account for any expenditure reasonably related to a political purpose. An inaugural celebration would be a permitted use of campaign funds under Section 89513(f)(2), which enumerates expenditure for an election victory celebration or similar campaign event.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether funds contributed to a candidate's campaign account can be used to finance an inaugural celebration, and whether contributions designated for inaugural costs must be deposited into the campaign account.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that contributions designated for inaugural costs must be deposited into the campaign account and may be used for the inaugural celebration, as such expenditures are permissible campaign fund uses under the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains clear question and conclusion sections. No section headers labeled as such, but question and conclusion content are clearly delineated. This is a confirmation of prior telephone advice. References Government Code sections and regulations throughout.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You stated that a number of corporations and individuals have made contributions to the Mayor's campaign fund and that a portion of that fund will be used for an inaugural celebration. You inquired whether or not the campaign account would be the proper repository for contributed funds that were specifically designated for the inaugural and whether or not an inaugural celebration could be financed with campaign account funds.

CONCLUSION: A contribution earmarked for inaugural costs would be a contribution for political purposes which would be required to be deposited into the candidate's campaign account. Generally, a candidate may use the funds in the campaign account for any expenditure reasonably related to a political purpose. An inaugural celebration would be a permitted use of campaign funds under Section 89513(f)(2), which enumerates expenditure for an election victory celebration or similar campaign event.

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC advice letter confirming telephone advice regarding the proper handling of contributions designated for Mayor Beverly O'Neill's inaugural celebration. The FPPC concluded that such contributions must be deposited into the campaign account and may be expended for the inaugural celebration as a permitted campaign-related activity.
